codeReview.summary: The fix commit cleanly removes the only remaining out-of-scope valuation truthsuite edit that blocked `catalog-contract-cleanup-and-proof`. `git show 385cfadd -- erp-domain/src/test/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/truthsuite/runtime/TS_RuntimeInventoryValuationExecutableCoverageTest.java` shows the catalog packet introduced the unrelated `LocalDate` import and `snapshotAsOf(...)` call, while `git show d443d044 -- .../TS_RuntimeInventoryValuationExecutableCoverageTest.java` removes those exact lines and restores `service.currentSnapshot(company)`. `git diff --name-status d7e11f0c..d443d044` changes only that file, so the in-scope catalog cleanup/docs/OpenAPI work from the failed feature remains intact, and `git diff --exit-code c2bf6503^ d443d044 -- .../TS_RuntimeInventoryValuationExecutableCoverageTest.java` confirms the file is back to its pre-packet state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to use an isolated git worktree to land this one-file remediation because the shared mission checkout already contained unrelated validation-artifact edits, but the packet workflow guidance does not document that worktree path as an approved way to keep a fix commit scoped and non-disruptive.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/.factory/skills/catalog-surface-worker/SKILL.md` describes the standard shared-checkout flow only; the fix transcript skeleton shows `git -C "/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control" worktree add -b "worker/catalog-contract-cleanup-valuation-revert" "/tmp/catalog-contract-cleanup-valuation-revert" HEAD`, and the handoff for session `991df618-0665-46b6-b884-d27258ca5e76` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` with the explanation that the shared checkout was already dirty from unrelated `.factory/validation/**` files.

summary: Pass: `d443d04482abe8296a4c48dc23dbe2551996fa89` removes the unrelated `TS_RuntimeInventoryValuationExecutableCoverageTest` band-aid, restores that file to its pre-packet state, and is the only delta on top of the already-reviewed catalog cleanup commit, so it clears the prior scrutiny blocker without disturbing the in-scope catalog contract cleanup work.
